chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH AT KARACH C.P.Nos.D-1920, 1921, 1922, 1923, 1924 & 1925 of 2024 _______________________________________________________________ Date Order with Signature of Judge _______________________________________________________________ C.P.No.D-1920 of 2024 KF Food Complex (Pvt.) Limited. C.P.No.D-1921 of 2024 MIK Industries (Pvt.) Limited. C.P.No.D-1922 of 2024 Inqlab Enterprises (Pvt.) Limited. C.P.No.D-1923 of 2024 Salam Food Industries (Pvt.) Limited. C.P.No.D-1924 of 2024 Margina Tea (Pvt.) Limited. C.P.No.D-1925 of 2024 ZRM Foods (Pvt.) Limited..................................Petitioners Versus Federation of Pakistan & others..........................Respondents 17-12-2024. Mr.Ahmed Ali Hussain, Advocate for the Petitioners. Mr.Rana Sakhawat, Advocate holding brief for Mr.Khalid Mehmood Rajpar, Advocate for Respondent (F.B.R) Ms.Alizeh Bashir, Assistant Attorney General. --- 1. Urgency granted. Learned counsel for the petitioners states that show cause notices have been issued to the petitioners in the respective petitions pertaining to subject matter. Learned counsel for the petitioners submits that the petitioners shall appear before the concerned Forum and respond to the show cause notice in accordance with law. Learned counsel for the petitioners states that it may be just and proper for the respondent Department to adjudicate the show cause notice after providing proper opportunity of hearing to the petitioners and decide the same while passing a speaking order, preferably within a period of 30 days. It is further stated that it would be just and proper for the respondent Department not to take any coercive action pending adjudication of the show cause notices.

Learned counsel for the petitioners states that it may be just and proper for the respondent Department to adjudicate the show cause notice after providing proper opportunity of hearing to the petitioners and decide the same while passing a speaking order, preferably within a period of 30 days. It is further stated that it would be just and proper for the respondent Department not to take any coercive action pending adjudication of the show cause notices. Learned counsel for the respondent Department submits that he has no cavil if the petitions are disposed of as aforesaid. Order accordingly. Copy of this order shall be place in each petition listed above. Judge Judge Nasir
